question_text: Which of the following statements about moments and levers is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: A moment is the total amount of force acting on an object.

options.label: B
options.text: A moment is the turning effect of a force around a pivot.

options.label: C
options.text: A moment is only produced when a force pushes an object in a straight line.

options.label: D
options.text: A moment increases if the pivot point moves closer to where the force is applied.

explanation: A moment is defined as the turning effect of a force around a fixed point (pivot). The further the force is applied from the pivot, the larger the moment produced. Options A and C describe force, not moment; option D is backwards—moving the pivot closer to the force reduces the distance and decreases the moment.
